cves.description: Supplemental group inheritance grants unintended access to GID 0 because of the lack of supplemental groups from mod_sql. When no supplemental groups are provided by mod_sql, the process defaults to inheriting the root group (GID 0), allowing privilege escalation.
cves.related_attack_surface: If mod_sql is used for authentication and supplemental groups are not explicitly configured, authenticated users may gain root group access. Test with mod_sql authentication to check if GID 0 is inherited.

cves.description: make_ftp_cmd in main.c has a one-byte out-of-bounds read and daemon crash due to mishandling of quote/backslash semantics. Sending specially crafted FTP commands with malformed quoting can crash the daemon.
cves.related_attack_surface: Although fixed, the FTP command parsing logic in main.c remains an attack surface. Fuzz FTP commands with unusual quote/backslash combinations to test for similar parsing issues.

cves.affected_versions: ProFTPD with mod_sftp using OpenSSH extensions before SSH 9.6 mitigations
cves.description: The SSH transport protocol with certain OpenSSH extensions allows remote attackers to bypass integrity checks such that some packets are omitted from the extension negotiation message, enabling a Terrapin prefix truncation attack that can down[REDACTED] connection security.
cves.related_attack_surface: If mod_sftp is enabled, test whether the server supports strict key exchange (kex) to mitigate Terrapin. Check SSH cipher negotiation for ChaCha20-Poly1305 or CBC with Encrypt-then-MAC which are vulnerable modes.

cves.description: mod_radius copies blocks of 16 characters, which can cause memory disclosure to RADIUS servers. Sensitive memory contents may leak to the authentication backend.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed in 1.3.7c. If mod_radius is compiled/loaded, verify the fix is present. The pattern of fixed-size memory copies remains worth testing.

cves.description: Memory pool corruption via interrupted data transfer channel triggers a use-after-free in alloc_pool. An authenticated attacker can interrupt a data transfer while the data channel is still open, corrupting the memory pool and potentially achieving remote code execution.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed in 1.3.7a, but memory pool management remains a critical attack surface. Test by rapidly interrupting data transfers (ABOR during LIST/RETR/STOR) to probe for pool corruption variants.

cves.description: Out-of-bounds read vulnerability in mod_cap via the cap_to_text function. Caused by an outdated bundled libcap version. Can lead to information disclosure or crash.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed by using system libcap. Verify whether 1.3.9 still bundles its own cap_text.c or uses system library.

cves.type: NULL Pointer Dereference / Denial of Service
cves.description: NULL pointer dereference in tls_verify_crl function when processing CRL validation, causing daemon crash.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but TLS CRL handling remains an attack surface if TLS client authentication is enabled.

cves.description: Wrong iteration variable in CRL checking causes some CRL entries to be ignored during certificate validation, potentially allowing revoked certificates to be accepted.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but CRL validation logic is worth testing if TLS client certificates are used.

cves.description: Incorrect CRL field checking (checks subject twice instead of subject and issuer) prevents some valid CRLs from being applied, allowing clients with revoked certificates to connect.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but TLS certificate validation remains a potential attack surface.

cves.type: NULL Pointer Dereference / Denial of Service
cves.description: NULL pointer dereference when sk_X509_REVOKED_value() returns NULL for an empty CRL. Crashes the daemon when validating client certificates against an empty CRL.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but represents the pattern of insufficient NULL checks in TLS handling code.

cves.description: Remote unauthenticated denial-of-service via overly long FTP commands that cause a child process to enter an infinite loop (CWE-835). No authentication required.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but command length handling in main.c remains worth fuzzing. Send extremely long command lines to test boundary conditions.

cves.type: Arbitrary File Copy / Improper Access Control
cves.description: mod_copy does not honor <Limit READ> and <Limit WRITE> configuration directives. Authenticated users (including anonymous if enabled) can use SITE CPFR/CPTO commands to copy any file on the server, bypassing access controls. Related to CVE-2015-3306.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but mod_copy SITE CPFR/CPTO commands are still available if mod_copy is loaded. Test whether access controls are properly enforced in 1.3.9. Verify <Limit> directives work correctly with mod_copy.

cves.description: AllowChrootSymlinks only checks the last path component, allowing attackers with local access to bypass chroot restrictions by replacing intermediate path components with symbolic links.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but symlink handling in chroot environments remains an attack surface. Test path traversal via symlinks in intermediate directories.

cves.description: Improper handling of TLSDHParamFile directive might cause a weaker than intended Diffie-Hellman key to be used, potentially allowing cryptographic attacks.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but TLS configuration and key exchange parameters remain worth auditing. Check DH parameter strength.

cves.type: Arbitrary File Read/Write / Remote Code Execution
cves.affected_versions: ProFTPD 1.3.5 (and potentially earlier versions with mod_copy)
cves.description: The mod_copy module allows UNAUTHENTICATED remote attackers to read and write arbitrary files via SITE CPFR and SITE CPTO commands. This can be chained with web server document roots to achieve remote code execution (e.g., copying /etc/passwd or writing PHP shells).
cves.related_attack_surface: The original unauthenticated variant is fixed, but mod_copy functionality persists. If mod_copy is loaded, test SITE CPFR/CPTO with various path traversal attempts and access control configurations.

cves.description: Integer overflow in kbdint.c in mod_sftp allows remote attackers to cause denial of service via a crafted keyboard-interactive authentication request.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but mod_sftp keyboard-interactive authentication remains an attack surface for integer handling issues.

cves.description: Race condition in the MKD/XMKD command handling allows local attackers to perform symlink attacks on newly created directories.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but directory creation race conditions are a general class worth testing.

cves.description: Use-after-free vulnerability in the Response API allows remote authenticated users to execute arbitrary code via vectors involving an error that occurs after an FTP data transfer.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but the Response API and error handling during data transfers remain attack surfaces. Test error conditions during active transfers.

cves.description: Integer overflow in mod_sftp causes memory exhaustion, leading to denial of service.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but mod_sftp integer handling remains an attack surface.

cves.description: A malicious backdoor was embedded in the official ProFTPD 1.3.3c source tarball. The backdoor implements a hidden FTP command that triggers arbitrary shell command execution with root privileges.
cves.related_attack_surface: Not applicable to 1.3.9 (supply chain compromise specific to 2010). However, verify source integrity of any ProFTPD build.

cves.description: Heap-based buffer overflow in sql_prepare_where function in contrib/mod_sql.c when mod_sql is enabled. Crafted usernames containing substitution tags are not properly handled during SQL query construction, allowing denial of service and possible code execution.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but mod_sql username handling and substitution tag processing remain worth fuzzing.

cves.type: Stack-Based Buffer Overflow / Remote Code Execution
cves.description: Multiple stack-based buffer overflows in pr_netio_telnet_gets function in netio.c. Remote attackers can execute arbitrary code by sending TELNET IAC escape characters to the FTP or FTPS server. No authentication required.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but Telnet IAC handling in the FTP control channel remains an attack surface. Send IAC sequences embedded in FTP commands.

cves.description: Multiple directory traversal vulnerabilities in mod_site_misc allow remote authenticated users to create directories, delete directories, create symlinks, and modify file timestamps via directory traversal sequences in SITE MKDIR, SITE RMDIR, SITE SYMLINK, and SITE UTIME commands.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but if mod_site_misc is loaded, SITE commands with path traversal sequences remain worth testing.

cves.description: The mod_tls module does not properly handle a null character in a domain name in the Subject Alternative Name field of an X.509 client certificate when dNSNameRequired is enabled, allowing hostname restriction bypass.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but TLS certificate validation remains an attack surface.

cves.description: SQL injection vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands via a '%' (percent) character in the username, which introduces a single quote during variable substitution by mod_sql.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but mod_sql variable substitution in usernames remains an attack surface. Test with special characters (%, ', ") in usernames.

cves.description: With NLS support enabled, attackers can bypass SQL injection protections via invalid encoded multibyte characters that are not properly handled by mod_sql_mysql and mod_sql_postgres.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but multibyte character handling in SQL modules remains an attack surface. Test with various character encodings in authentication fields.

cves.description: CPU exhaustion via ABOR command sent during active data transfers. The ABOR handling can consume excessive CPU resources.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but ABOR during transfers is a general pattern worth testing for resource exhaustion.

cves.description: Buffer overflow in the tls_x509_name_oneline function in mod_tls module allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a large data length argument in a TLS certificate.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but TLS certificate parsing remains an attack surface. Test with oversized certificate fields.

cves.description: Stack-based buffer overflow in pr_ctrls_recv_request function in mod_ctrls allows local users with Controls ACL access to execute arbitrary code via a large reqarglen value, potentially achieving root privileges.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but mod_ctrls local socket interface remains an attack surface if the controls socket is accessible.

cves.type: Stack-Based Buffer Overflow / Remote Code Execution
cves.description: Stack-based buffer overflow in the sreplace function allows remote attackers (probably authenticated) to cause denial of service and execute arbitrary code.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but the sreplace function may still exist. Test with long strings containing substitution patterns.

cves.description: Multiple format string vulnerabilities allow attackers to cause denial of service or obtain sensitive information via (1) certain inputs to the shutdown message from ftpshut, or (2) the SQLShowInfo mod_sql directive.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but format string handling in display messages and SQL directives remains a pattern to watch. Test with format specifiers (%s, %x, %n) in user-controlled fields.

cves.description: Off-by-one buffer overflow in the _xlate_ascii_write function during ASCII mode file transfers.
cves.related_attack_surface: Fixed, but ASCII mode transfer handling remains an attack surface.

patterns: PATTERN 1 - mod_copy access control failures: CVE-2015-3306 and CVE-2019-12815 show that mod_copy has repeatedly failed to enforce access controls on SITE CPFR/CPTO commands. The module allows file copy operations that bypass <Limit> directives. Even in 1.3.9, if mod_copy is loaded, its interaction with access controls should be thoroughly tested.

PATTERN 2 - Memory safety issues in core pool/response handling: CVE-2020-9273 (use-after-free in pool.c) and CVE-2011-4130 (use-after-free in Response API) show recurring memory management issues, particularly during error conditions in data transfers. ProFTPD's custom memory pool allocator is a high-value target.

PATTERN 3 - Buffer overflows in string handling functions: CVE-2006-5815 (sreplace), CVE-2010-4221 (pr_netio_telnet_gets), CVE-2010-4652 (sql_prepare_where), CVE-2006-6170 (tls_x509_name_oneline), and CVE-2023-51713 (make_ftp_cmd) demonstrate recurring buffer overflow patterns in string processing. The FTP command parser is a persistent attack surface.

PATTERN 4 - mod_sql injection and substitution bugs: CVE-2009-0542, CVE-2009-0543, and CVE-2010-4652 show that mod_sql has had multiple issues with variable substitution, character encoding, and SQL query construction. Username fields processed by mod_sql are a recurring injection vector.

PATTERN 5 - TLS/certificate handling weaknesses: CVE-2009-3639, CVE-2016-3125, CVE-2019-19269, CVE-2019-19270, CVE-2019-19271, CVE-2019-19272, and CVE-2006-6170 show a pattern of vulnerabilities in mod_tls, particularly in certificate validation and CRL checking. TLS implementations in ProFTPD have been a persistent source of bugs.

PATTERN 6 - mod_sftp integer handling: CVE-2011-1137 and CVE-2013-4359 show integer overflow issues in mod_sftp that lead to denial of service. SSH protocol handling modules have their own class of vulnerabilities.

PATTERN 7 - Denial of service via malformed input: CVE-2019-18217 (infinite loop), CVE-2023-51713 (OOB read crash), CVE-2001-0136 (memory leak), and CVE-2008-7265 (CPU exhaustion) show that ProFTPD has had multiple DoS vulnerabilities triggered by malformed or edge-case inputs requiring no authentication.

PATTERN 8 - Directory traversal in SITE commands: CVE-2010-3867 shows that SITE commands (MKDIR, RMDIR, SYMLINK, UTIME) in mod_site_misc were vulnerable to path traversal. Any module implementing SITE subcommands is a potential traversal target.
PATTERN 9 - Symlink-related bypasses: CVE-2017-7418 and CVE-2012-6095 show that symlink handling has been a source of access control bypasses, particularly in chroot environments.
priority_targets: mod_copy (SITE CPFR/CPTO): Historically the most exploited ProFTPD vulnerability class. Even if fixed, test that access controls (<Limit>) are properly enforced in 1.3.9. If mod_copy is loaded and anonymous access is enabled, this is critical.

FTP command parser (main.c): CVE-2023-51713 showed OOB read in make_ftp_cmd, which was fixed before 1.3.8a but indicates the command parsing code has had recent issues. Fuzz with unusual quoting, backslashes, and extremely long commands.

mod_sql authentication: CVE-2024-48651 (GID 0 inheritance) may affect 1.3.9 depending on build. Test supplemental group [REDACTED] when authenticating via mod_sql. Also fuzz username/password fields for SQL injection variants.
Memory pool allocator (pool.c): The pool allocator has been the source of use-after-free vulnerabilities. Test by interrupting data transfers at various stages (ABOR, connection reset, timeout).
mod_tls certificate handling: TLS implementation has had 7+ CVEs. Test with malformed certificates, oversized fields, null bytes in names, and empty CRLs.
mod_sftp (if enabled): Integer overflows, Terrapin attack (CVE-2023-48795). Test keyboard-interactive auth with edge-case values and verify strict key exchange support.
SITE command handling: Directory traversal via SITE MKDIR/RMDIR/SYMLINK/UTIME in mod_site_misc. Test all SITE subcommands with ../ sequences.
Telnet IAC in FTP commands: CVE-2010-4221 was critical. Though fixed, embed IAC escape sequences (0xFF) in FTP command streams to test for handling issues.

Data transfer error handling: Multiple CVEs (CVE-2020-9273, CVE-2011-4130, CVE-2008-7265) relate to errors during data transfers. Test ABOR, connection drops, and timeout conditions during active transfers.

Anonymous access + mod_copy: If anonymous FTP is enabled with mod_copy loaded, this combination has historically been the most dangerous configuration. Test unauthenticated and anonymous SITE CPFR/CPTO.
